ety: bhāg˚ = Prk. भग्गे(इ), or भग्ग(इ), fr. भग्ग = S. भग्न, p.p.p. of rt. भंज्
def: v.n. To run, to run off or away, fly, flee, take flight, escape, abscond, make oneself scarce
more: v. n. S. भाज् to separate. 1. To run away; fly; flee; make off; give leg bail. 1. Āṅdhī āë baiṭh jāë, meṅh āë bhāg jāë . Prov. In a storm sit down, when it rains run away. 2. Tānā bānā jal * gayā, jalā nahīṅ ek tāgā, Ghar kā chor pakaṛ līyā, ghar morī meṅ ko bhāgā . Riddle. Ans. jāl a net. Both web and woof thrown in the jal , no single thread would burn, In it the thieves were caught, their house did through the drain excern. 3. Bijulik māral, luāṭh d ekhe bhāge . Prov. Singed by lightning he runs from a burning stick. 4. Āyā Ramzān, bhāgā Shaitān . Mah. Prov. When Ramzan (Mah. Lent) comes, the Devil flies. 5. Ūṅṭ jab bhāge, jab pachchham ko . Prov. When the camel bolts, it is to the West (to Arabia). (Home has the first place in the mind). 2. To elope or run away with. 3. To escape; abscond; make or effect one's escape. 4. To slip, steal, slink away; pack off; beat a retreat; turn tail…
v. n. To flee, to run away.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀪𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀢𑀺 (*bhaggati), a verbal derivation from Sanskrit भग्न (bhagna, “broken, defeated, lost”), from the root भञ्ज् (bhañj). Cognate with Nepali भाग्नु (bhāgnu), Gujarati ભાગવું (bhāgvũ), as well as Punjabi ਭੱਜਣਾ (bhajjaṇā) from a different derivation.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Good, excellent, virtuous, righteous; honest, respectable; benevolent, kind; healthy, well, sound; fortunate, prosperous; strange, wonderful, admirable; comical, droll; — adv. & intj. Well, very good; how fortunate! forsooth, in sooth, of a truth; strange; — s.m. Good, welfare, &c. ( = bhalāʼī, q.v.)
more: good. See اچّها adj. adv. and intj. 1. Bhale kā bhalā . Prov. Good yields good. 2. Bhale kī bāteṅ ras kī khān, Bure kī bāteṅ dukh nidhān . Prov. The words of good men are a hive of nectar sweet, The words of bad men are a nest of stings replete. bhale bure , n. m. Bad men; bad characters; rogues. bhalā burā kahnā, bhalī burī sunānā , v. a. To speak ill of; abuse; insult; reproach; inveigh against; rail at; vilify. bhalā chaṅggā , n. m. bhalī chaṅggī , n. f. See achchhā bichchhā. Bhalī chaṅgī apnī na kho jān tū, Ki woh hai parī, aur insān tū . Mir Hasan. bhalā karnā, bhalī karnā , v. a. See achchhā k . (1, 2). 1. Chaṛh jā beṭā sūlī par, Rām bhalī kareṅge! Prov. Come on my son, impale yourself, Ram will reward the sacrifice! (Said of sinister advice). 2. Bhalā kīyā so Khudā ne, burā kīyā so bande ne . Prov. Good is from God, evil from man. bhale ko , intj. How fortunate! how lucky! Be-jurm…

adj. 1. Good, excellent, benevolent, kind, healthy, well, virtuous, righteous, sound. 2. Strange, wonderful, admirable, comical, droll. بهلاآدمي bhalā ādmī, s. m. A person of respectability, a gentleman; ( iron. ) a silly fellow. بهلاماننا bhalā mānnā, To take well.
subs: s.m. All sorts of people; bad men, bad characters

intj. Well-said! you don't say so! strange! no matter!
v.n. To appear or look well; to become, &c. ( = aććhā lagnā), q.v.
bhalā mānas, s.m. Courteous, polite, or humane person; good, honest, or respectable man; gentleman; man of position or rank, nobleman; (ironic.) silly fellow, simpleton, fool; wicked fellow, rascal
v.t. To take well, take in good part.

def: adj. In good order, in health, perfect, hale, well.

n. m. 1. A courteous, polite, humane person; a good, or respectable man; a gentleman. 1. Jis ke pās nahīṅ paisā, woh bhalā mānas kaisā? Prov. Who poor beseem a gentleman none deem. 2. Adhan de-ke khojat rahalo, kehū na delā mohī pāiṅch rekī, Ser bhar chaūrā gotini mohe dīhalī, ūhī meṅ bhaīlo bhal mānas rekī! Wom. Bhoj. Song. The water set upon the fire grown hot, Some rice in vain on credit now I sought, A relative did then give me a seer, Which made me quite respectable appear! 2. A simple, meek, gentle, inoffensive person. Bhale mānas kī sab tarah kharābī hai . Prov. The good and pure all ills endure. (He is maligned whether he returns abuse for abuse, or bears it meekly). 3. A man of rank; a noble; a grandee. 4. ( Ironic .) A fool; a wicked person; a rascal. Are, to bhale mānas, mān. bhalā mānnā , v. n. To take in good part.
s. f. Goodness, good health, welfare.

Humanity; benevolence. See ashrāfat. Bhalmansāt kā zamānā hī nahīṅ . Prov. There is no humanity in this age.
subs: (-se, and -ke sāth), To show or practise beneficence (towards), to be good or kind (to), &c.

It is also used for making wales on the body, which are shown in a criminal court as evidence of having been beaten).

def: v.n. To be approved (of), to be acceptable (to, -ko), be pleasing (to), to please; to be beloved, be held dear; to suit, fit, become; to seem good or befitting (syn. aććhā lagnā).
more: v. n. S. भद् to be pleased. See اچهالگنا (1, 4). 1. Samdhan ko hāthī kā bhāve āchhā, nīkā, pūrā, rī, Raṅg raṅgīlā, aur chaṭkīlā hāthī dāṅt kā chūṛā, rī . (A buse of the bridegroom's mother). Marriage song. Sīṭnā , q. v. An elephant's, nice, full, the mother of the groom would like, Bright shining ivory bangles which the eye should strike. To be beloved, or held dear; to be a favorite. 2. Tauṅ to bhaē gaī utrī chhatrī, maiṅ pī ke man bhāī sūṅ, Tere bāp ne daī āp se, māṅ mannat sūṅ āī sūṅ . W. Rus. song. Saukan . Your nose is out of joint, my husband's favorite am I, Your father gave you him, with much entreaty sought was I. (The new co-wife ( sautan ) to the first wife ( biyāhtā ).
v. n. To be approved of, to please, to suit, to fit.
v. a. (dakh.) To put, place, let go, extend, pour out, cast (?).

s. f. A low sound, a distant sound, hum.
subs: v.n. Ring or din to be heard, to catch or hear the sound (of).

n. m. S. भंज् to break. Breaking up; dissolution. bhanḍ kharābā, bhanḍak bhanḍā , n. m. A breaking up; dissolution; destruction; ruin. bhanḍ kar'nā , v. a. 1. To break; destroy; cut short; put a stop to. 2. To spoil; mar; throw cold water upon. 1. Sharāb unko kahiṅ mat pilāīyo, Inshā, Woh mast ho-ho-ke majlis ko bhanḍ karte haiṅ . Inshā. 2. Nahiṅ ṭalūṅ din rāt hāṭ se, baje kaṛākaṛ ḍanḍā, Ghaṛī ek meṅ dekh tamāshā, kar dūṅ bhanḍak bhanḍā . Sutre Shāhī. Nor night nor day this mart I'll leave, on drumsticks 'Ill play on, In half an hour you'll see some fun, some bloody work'll be done. bhaṅḍ honā , v. n. To be spoiled; broken; destroyed.

n. m. S. भणड् to jest. 1. A native player or actor, usually a Mahomedan; a buffoon. Mahfil vīrān jahāṅ bhāṅḍ na bāshad, Jaṅgal vīrān jahāṅ sher na-bāshad . Players. Dull is the assembly where the player's not, The forest where the king of beasts is not. 2. One who cannot keep a secret; a telltale.
s.m. Earthen pot or vessel (for holding grain, &c.); estate; equipage
more: s. m. 1. A large earthen pot. 2. Estate, equipage. بهانڐاپهوٿنا bhānḍā-phūṭnā, v. n. To lose oneʼs character.
subs: (-kā), To leak out, be disclosed (a secret); to lose one's character

Feast of Jogis, Sanyāsīs, &c.
more: s. m. A place where household goods are kept, a storehouse.

def: n. m. S. भाण्डागार from भाण्ड a vessel. 1. The bed of a river. 2. A receptacle for water; a tank or reservoir, particularly one which supplies a fountain. 3. Storehouse; godown; warehouse. Bābā kā bhanḍārā bhar-pūr rahe! Mendicants' cry. May your store house ever full remain, good Sir! 4. The well of a native cart. 5. Head; skull ( khoprī ). Ek lā hī meṅ bhanḍār khul gayā . 6. Villages managed by the Rājā or zamīṅdār himself; his own peculiar estate, not rented or farmed to others. 7. A feast of jogīs, Sanyāsīs , etc. Mhāre bhanḍāre meṅ sājhā kar le, māī, mhāre bhanḍāre meṅ! Mendicants' cry. Secure a share, mother, in my storehouse (in the next world)!

wety: Inherited from Sanskrit भङ्ग (bhaṅga). Doublet of بَنْگ (baṅg) via Persian.

def: s.f. Hemp, &c. = bhāṅg, q.v.
more: n. f. S. भङ्गा. An intoxicating drug or potion made from hemp-leaves. 1. Kūṅḍī khutke ko bajā, aur dekh ṭuk qudrat ke khel, Chhoṛ sab kāmoṅ ko, gāfil, bhaṅg pī aur ḍanḍ pel . Nazīr. With pestle and mortar grind, and then see Nature's sportive play, Leave other things, thou fool! drink bhaṅg and exercise essay. 2. Sabaj raṅg meṅhdī par dhāve, kar chhūat akhiyan par āwe, Baiṭhat, uṭhat maṛoṛat aṅg. 'E sakhī, sājun'? 'Nā, sakhī, bhaṅg .' Riddle. Mukrī . Its green the myrtle's doth excel, the eyes a touch inflames, Now up, now down the body twists. 'No sweetheart', hemp proclaims. 3. Bhaṅgan, khush-raṅgan, fil-hāl chuppam chuppā . Bhaṅgaṛs' rhyme. Bhaṅg! liquor lovely green! but now all silent and serene! bhaṅg kar'nā, bhanjan k . v. a. S. भंज् to break. 1. To break; mutilate; destroy. 2. To spoil; mar; undo. 3. To invalidate; make null or void; abolish. bhaṅg honā , v. n. To be broken, spoi…
1. (for भंग r. भंज Break) s. m. Breaking, splitting; defeat, discomfiture. 2. (for भग्न ) adj. Broken, torn, overcome, defeated.
subs: s.m. The stick with which hemp-leaves are rubbed down to make the beverage called bhaṅg.

With pestle dont you get it just red-hot? 2. Kahtī hai āshnā se kyoṅ, mere yār, bhaṅgyā, Tū āj bhī na lāyā merī raṅgā-ke aṅgyā . Nazīr. bhaṅgeṛ-khānā , n. m. A bhang or liquor shop. bhaṅgyā'nā , v. n. To be intoxicated. Āj to, ākā, bhaṅgyāë huë mālūm hote ho .

wety: Feminine of بھان٘جا (bhāñjā, “nephew”), from Prakrit 𑀪𑀸𑀬𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚 (bhāyaṇijja) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit भागिनेय (bhāginēya).

wety: Feminine of بھان٘جا (bhāñjā, “nephew”), from Prakrit 𑀪𑀸𑀬𑀡𑀺𑀚𑁆𑀚 (bhāyaṇijja) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit भागिनेय (bhāginēya).
